name: Chinese Barbequed Pork from Kaitlin Young
recipeInstructions:
VARIATIONS:
Thai Barbeque Pork: Substitute fish sauce for soy sauce, and lime juice for sherry. Increase ginger to 1 T. Substitute 1 T chopped fresh coriander for five-spice powder.
Szechuan Barbequed Pork: Substitute 1 tbsp Chinese chili paste for five-spice powder. Add 1 green onion, chopped.
3. Trim any fat off the tenderloins; tuck ends under and tie each with a kitchen string. Place in a shallow glass dish.
4. Marinade: Whisk together soy sauce, hoisin sauce, sherry, black bean sauce, ginger, sugar, garlic, sesame oil, and five-spice powder.
5. Pour marinade over tenderloins and turn to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or up to 24 hours; turn occasionally.
6. Let stand for 30 minutes at room temperature before cooking.
7. Place tenderloins on rack in roasting pan, reserving marinade. Pour 1 cup water into pan.
8. Bake, basting generously four times, in a 375°F oven for 30-35 minutes, or until meat thermometer inserted at a 20-degree angle registers 160°F and meat still has a hint of pink.
9. Remove to cutting board, and tent with foil. Let stand for 10 minutes.
10. Remove string. Using sharp knife, slice pork diagonally into thin slices.
